name: Full-Text Search with tsvector and tsquery
description: Build a powerful full-text search engine using PostgreSQL native tsvector columns, GIN indexes, and ranked query results.
prerequisites: A table with text content to search

steps.title: Add a tsvector column to your table
steps.description: Create a generated tsvector column that automatically stays in sync with the source text.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title, '') || ' ' || coalesce(body, ''))) STORED;"
steps.tip: GENERATED ALWAYS AS creates a stored computed column that updates automatically when title or body change.

steps.title: Create a GIN index for fast search
steps.description: Add a GIN index on the tsvector column for efficient full-text queries.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "CREATE INDEX idx_articles_search ON articles USING GIN (search_vector);"

steps.title: Run a basic full-text search
steps.description: Search for articles matching a text query with automatic stemming.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "SELECT id, title, ts_rank(search_vector, to_tsquery('english', 'database & performance')) AS rank FROM articles WHERE search_vector @@ to_tsquery('english', 'database & performance') ORDER BY rank DESC LIMIT 10;"

steps.title: Use websearch_to_tsquery for user-friendly input
steps.description: Convert natural language search input into a tsquery without manual operators.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "SELECT id, title FROM articles WHERE search_vector @@ websearch_to_tsquery('english', 'how to optimize database queries') ORDER BY ts_rank(search_vector, websearch_to_tsquery('english', 'how to optimize database queries')) DESC LIMIT 10;"
steps.tip: websearch_to_tsquery handles quoted phrases, OR, and - (NOT) operators like a web search engine.

steps.title: Generate search result snippets
steps.description: Highlight matching terms in search results with ts_headline.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -d mydb -c "SELECT id, title, ts_headline('english', body, to_tsquery('english', 'performance'), 'StartSel=, StopSel=, MaxWords=35, MinWords=15') AS snippet FROM articles WHERE search_vector @@ to_tsquery('english', 'performance') LIMIT 5;"

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# PostgreSQL Full-Text Search Setup
set -euo pipefail

DB="mydb"
USER="postgres"

echo "=== Setting up full-text search ==="
psql -U "$USER" -d "$DB" -c "
-- Add tsvector column (if table exists)
ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS search_vector tsvector
  GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
    setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title, '')), 'A') ||
    setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(body, '')), 'B')
  ) STORED;

-- Create GIN index
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_articles_search ON articles USING GIN (search_vector);
"

echo ""
echo "=== Testing search ==="
psql -U "$USER" -d "$DB" -c "
SELECT id, title,
       ts_rank(search_vector, q) AS rank,
       ts_headline('english', body, q, 'StartSel=**, StopSel=**, MaxWords=30') AS snippet
FROM articles, websearch_to_tsquery('english', 'database optimization') AS q
WHERE search_vector @@ q
ORDER BY rank DESC
LIMIT 5;
"

echo ""
echo "=== Search index stats ==="
psql -U "$USER" -d "$DB" -c "
SELECT pg_size_pretty(pg_relation_size('idx_articles_search')) AS index_size,
       (SELECT count(*) FROM articles) AS total_articles;
"

echo ""
echo "=== Available text search configurations ==="
psql -U "$USER" -d "$DB" -c "\dF+"

faq.question: What is the difference between GIN and GiST indexes for full-text search?
faq.answer: GIN indexes are faster for lookups and better for static data. GiST indexes are faster to build and update, better for frequently changing data. For most full-text search use cases, GIN is preferred.

faq.question: How do I search across multiple languages?
faq.answer: Use different text search configurations per language (e.g., 'english', 'french', 'german'). You can store a language column and use it to select the right configuration, or create multiple tsvector columns for multilingual content.

faq.question: How does tsvector weighting work?
faq.answer: setweight assigns importance levels A through D to tsvector entries. Weight A (title) ranks higher than B (body) in ts_rank calculations. This makes title matches more relevant than body matches in search results.
